heading: Uttering counterfeit obligations or securities
label: 472 Uttering counterfeit obligations or securities
text: Whoever, with intent to defraud, passes, utters, publishes, or sells, or attempts to pass, utter, publish, or sell, or with like intent brings into the United States or keeps in possession or conceals any falsely made, forged, counterfeited, or altered obligation or other security of the United States, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 20 years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 265 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 15135 Stat. 1116Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.Changes in phraseology were made.
Amendments2001— substituted “20 years” for “fifteen years”.Pub. L. 107–561994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $5,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
